description: Figure 20 presents a six-stage schematic mechanism illustrating the growth of small manganese oxide nodules during the oxidation of α and α+β-Mn Fe-Mn-Al alloys at 800°C in 200 torr pure oxygen. Stage 1 depicts void formation beneath the protective Al2O3 scale. Stage 2 shows the development of flaws or defects in the overlying alumina layer, allowing O2(g) ingress and Mn(g) vapor formation. Stage 3 illustrates outward growth of Mn2O3 nodules. Stage 4 details the "filling in" process where voids are filled via short-circuit diffusion of oxygen ions (O2-) and outward diffusion of Mn2+ ions, forming Mn3O4 within the void. Stage 5 shows the formation and growth of Al2O3 or (Fe,Mn)Al2O4 particles at the oxide-metal interface within the filled void. Finally, Stage 6 depicts the formation of a continuous healing layer and conversion of the nodule structure to Mn2O3. This schematic elucidates how manganese vapor transport through substrate voids and reaction with oxygen leads to nodule penetration of protective alumina scales, followed by aluminum internal oxidation that creates healing layers capable of reducing further growth rates.
